---
title: "Physical AI Toolchain"
subtitle: "Production-Ready Robotics on Azure with NVIDIA Isaac"
---

# Welcome {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Physical AI Toolchain** is an open-source, production-ready framework integrating
[Microsoft Azure](https://azure.microsoft.com/) cloud services with
[NVIDIA's](https://developer.nvidia.com/) physical AI stack — accelerating robotics
developers from data curation through training to edge deployment.
:::

![Physical AI spans the full lifecycle from simulation through cloud training to edge deployment.
*Image: [Robotic arm in manufacturing](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:KUKA_robot_for_welding.jpg) by KUKA Roboter GmbH, [CC BY-SA 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/)*](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5e/KUKA_robot_for_welding.jpg/640px-KUKA_robot_for_welding.jpg){fig-alt="Industrial robotic arm performing welding operations" width="80%"}

## What This Book Covers

This book is the comprehensive reference for deploying, training, and operating
physical AI workloads on Azure with NVIDIA Isaac. It covers:

| Capability | Description |
|---|---|
| **Simulation & Synthetic Data** | Isaac Sim/Lab environments for RL training and synthetic data generation |
| **Edge Data Capture** | ROS 2 recording on Jetson with chunking, compression, and cloud upload |
| **Cloud Data Pipeline** | Automated ROS-to-LeRobot conversion, quality validation, orchestration |
| **Training Infrastructure** | OSMO + Azure ML for scalable RL and IL training with experiment tracking |
| **Model Evaluation** | Offline replay, Isaac Sim validation, and evaluation dashboards |
| **Model Deployment** | ONNX/TensorRT conversion, container packaging, GitOps edge deployment |
| **Fleet Intelligence** | Drift detection, telemetry, and retraining triggers |

## Architecture Overview

The toolchain follows a **hub-spoke architecture** where Azure cloud services
form the hub and edge devices (NVIDIA Jetson) form the spokes:

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-architecture
%%| fig-cap: "Physical AI Toolchain — Hub and Spoke Architecture"
%%| fig-width: 100%
flowchart LR
    subgraph Edge["Edge (Jetson)"]
        DC[Data Capture]
        INF[Inference]
        TEL[Telemetry]
    end

    subgraph Azure["Azure Cloud"]
        AKS[AKS + GPU]
        AML[Azure ML]
        OSMO[NVIDIA OSMO]
        STOR[Blob Storage]
        ARC[Azure Arc]
    end

    subgraph Training["Training Pipeline"]
        RL[RL — Isaac Lab]
        IL[IL — LeRobot]
        MLF[MLflow Tracking]
    end

    DC -->|Upload| STOR
    STOR --> AML
    AML --> RL
    AML --> IL
    RL --> MLF
    IL --> MLF
    OSMO --> AKS
    MLF -->|Model Registry| ARC
    ARC -->|GitOps| INF
    INF --> TEL
    TEL -->|MQTT| Azure
```

## Who This Is For

- **Robotics researchers** moving from Isaac Sim prototypes to production
- **Platform engineers** standardizing physical AI pipelines across teams
- **Enterprise teams** piloting Jetson + Azure deployments with security and compliance

## Quick Example

Here is a minimal example of submitting a training job to Azure ML:

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false
# Submit an Isaac Lab RL training job
az ml job create \
  --file workflows/azureml/rl-training-job.yml \
  --resource-group rg-nvidia-dev-001 \
  --workspace-name mlw-nvidia-dev-001 \
  --set display_name="pick-place-rl-$(date +%Y%m%d)" \
  --set environment_variables.ACCEPT_EULA="Y"
```

```{python}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false
# Connect to MLflow experiment tracking
import mlflow

mlflow.set_tracking_uri("azureml://westus2.api.azureml.ms/mlflow/v1.0/subscriptions/{sub}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/{ws}")
mlflow.set_experiment("pick-place-rl")

with mlflow.start_run():
    mlflow.log_param("algorithm", "PPO")
    mlflow.log_param("framework", "skrl")
    mlflow.log_metric("reward_mean", 245.6)
```

## License

This project is licensed under the [MIT License](https://github.com/microsoft/physical-ai-toolchain/blob/main/LICENSE).
Documentation text is available under [CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/).
